fields.Identifying_Data_Chief_Complaint: 38-year-old African American male with schizophrenia with work problems.
fields.Subjective: Patient appears unkempt and irritable. Girlfriend reports he's been skipping clozapine doses 3-4 times weekly for past month because weekly blood draws are 'too much hassle' and he feels fine without it. Since inconsistent dosing, paranoid thoughts increased - believes warehouse supervisors conspiring to fire him and coworkers stealing from his locker. Hearing voices commenting on work performance multiple times daily. Sleep disrupted 4-5 hours. Appetite decreased, weight down 8 lbs. Received final warning at work last week for arguing with supervisor. Lives with girlfriend. Increasingly suspicious and argumentative at home. Mood 'everyone's against me' rated 3/10. Denies substance use but girlfriend suspects marijuana use. Denies SI/HI. (Seen by Dr. Marcus Freeman, MD, Psychiatrist)

Mental_Status_Examination.Thought_Content: Paranoid delusions about workplace conspiracy, auditory hallucinations

Laboratory_Data.Recent_Lab_Results: Clozapine level subtherapeutic at 180 ng/mL; ANC 3200 (acceptable).

fields.Diagnostic_Impression: Schizophrenia with moderate exacerbation due to irregular clozapine use.
fields.Psychosocial_Context: Lives with girlfriend; employed but job in jeopardy; possible substance use; relationship strain.
Assessment_and_Plan: Symptom worsening related to skipping clozapine 3-4 times weekly.
Discussed importance of daily dosing and weekly monitoring.
Girlfriend to supervise daily medication and accompany to lab draws.
Addressed possible marijuana use interfering with treatment.
Job at risk - may need workplace accommodations or FMLA.
Follow-up in 1 week with repeat clozapine level.
fields.Author: Note written by Dr. Marcus Freeman, MD, Psychiatrist — Industrial Workers Clinic, 2500 Labor Drive, Chicago, IL 60607, Phone: (555) 229-4421, Fax: (555) 229-4422
